name: Jadarian Price
headline: Rookie inherits Seattle's run-heavy backfield with lead-role upside
body:
Seattle spent a first-round-caliber pick on Price to take over for Kenneth Walker III, and the path to volume is unusually clean for a rookie. With Walker shipped to Kansas City and Zach Charbonnet's knee expected to sideline him into the early season, the only obstacles between Price and lead duties are journeymen Emanuel Wilson and George Holani. In a backfield this thin, an offense that profiles to stay ground-oriented hands him real touches from Week 1.

The talent backs it up: Price ranked 25th in yards after contact and 31st in breakaway rate across his Notre Dame years, the kind of contact balance and burst that travels. The catch is the passing game, where just 15 career college receptions and three years of minimal targets cap his PPR ceiling and could cost him third downs. Projections near 950 rushing yards and 7.9 scores justify a Round 5 seasonal price, with locked-in dynasty appeal as the second rookie back off the board.
